titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/258

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=244 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:39:31.075004+00 | translation moa=245 status=live modified=2021-04-04 14:39:35.432678+00

p.cl:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis ⁊ fidelibus ad quos presens scriptum peruenerit presentibus ⁊ futuris  Willelmus
filius Gamelli de tuinham salutem.

Vniuersitati vestre significo me consensu domini mei Alani filii Rollandi .
⁊ assensu Walteri filii ⁊ heredis mei dedisse ⁊ concessisse ⁊ hac carta mea confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte
crucis de edenburc ⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam totum ius
quod habere uidebar in ecclesia de Tuenham . ⁊ in perpetuum pro me ⁊ heredibus meis abrenunciasse clamori quem clamabam
in aduocatione ipsius ecclesie . Preterea do ⁊ concedo assensu ipsius Walteri filii mei predicte ecclesie ⁊ predictis canonicis
in perpetuam ellemosinam quatuor acras terre in territorio eiusdem ville . eiusdem diuisis per quas ego ipsam terram in consepectu
prudentum virorum Ipsis canonicis tenendas perambulaui ⁊ domum una in ipsa uilla de qua predictos cano
nicos saisiaui predicte ecclesie do ⁊ concedo cum communi pastura eiusdem uille ad sexdecim aueria ⁊ ad unum
equum . ⁊ cum omnibus aliis communibus aisiamentis eiusdem uille . Quare volo ut predictam ecclesiam prenominati ca
nonici cum omnibus iustis pertinenciis suis libere ⁊ quiete in perpetuam elemosinam ⁊ puram teneant in perpe
tuum . ⁊ possideant.

Hiis testibus . Siluestro . ⁊ Andrea . ⁊ thoma capellanis . Matheo decano . Roberto
clerico . Roberto halfchniht . Roberto ⁊ Gamello fratribus . Roberto filio Matildis . ⁊ aliis quam pluribus .
To all sons and faithful of Holy Mother Church to whom the present deed shall come, present and future, William son of Gamel of Twynholm sends Greeting:

I make known to you all that I, with the consent of my lord, Alan son of Roland, and the agreement of Walter my son and heir, have given and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the whole right that I thought good to have in the church of Twynholm, and I have renounced in perpetuity, for myself and my heirs, the claim that I claimed in the advowson of that church. Furthermore I give and grant, with the assent of Walter my son himself, to the foresaid church and the foresaid canons, in perpetual alms, four acres of land in the territory of the same toun, for the canons themselves to hold the same bounds by which I perambulated that very land in the sight of worthy men. And to the foresaid church I give and grant one dwelling in the toun itself, in respect of which I have put the foresaid canons in possession, with common pasture of the same toun for sixteen draught animals and for one horse, and with all other common easements of the same toun. Wherefore I will that the aforenamed canons should hold and possess in perpetuity the foresaid church with all its just pertinents, freely and quietly in perpetual and pure alms.
With these witnesses, Silvester, Andrew and Thomas, chaplains, Matthew the dean, Robert the clerk, Robert Halfknight, Robert and Gamel, brothers, Robert son of Matilda, and very many others.
